2606.12318.title: Harness In-Context Operator Learning with Chain of Operators
2606.12318.paper_summary: Neural operators approximate mappings between function spaces, but often generalize poorly to other operators and usually require fine-tuning or retraining. In-Context Operator Networks (ICON) addresses this issue by prompting the model with numerical context so that the model learns specific operators from prompts and adapt to different operators without fine-tuning. However, ICON may still fail to generalize to out-of-distribution (OOD) operator tasks. Inpired by the success of harness engineering of Large Language models (LLMs), we introduce Chain of Operators (CHOP), a framework that harness a frozen ICON to OOD operator tasks without updating its parameters. Specifically, CHOP constructs a chain of operators consisting of explicit elementary transformations and the frozen ICON. Experiments on a scalar conservation law and a mean-field control problem show that CHOP reduces relative inference error over direct ICON evaluation, while each operator in the chain remains interpretable and in closed form. A chain constructed on one PDE family further generalizes to a different family, indicating shared mechanisms across harness systems.
2606.12318.paper_summary_zh: 神經運算子近似函數空間之間的映射，但通常對其他運算子的泛化能力較差，並且通常需要微調或重新訓練。上下文運算子網絡（ICON）通過用數值上下文提示模型來解決這個問題，使模型能夠從提示中學習特定運算子並適應不同的運算子，而無需微調。然而，ICON 仍可能無法對分佈外（OOD）運算子任務進行泛化。受到大型語言模型（LLMs）工程成功的啟發，我們引入了運算子鏈（CHOP），這是一個框架，利用一個凍結的 ICON 來處理 OOD 運算子任務，而無需更新其參數。具體而言，CHOP 構建了一個由明確的基本變換和凍結的 ICON 組成的運算子鏈。在一個標量守恆定律和一個均值場控制問題上的實驗顯示，CHOP 在直接 ICON 評估上減少了相對推理誤差，同時鏈中的每個運算子仍然可解釋且以封閉形式存在。基於一個 PDE 家族構建的鏈進一步泛化到不同的家族，表明跨越各種系統的共享機制。
2606.12318.author: Minghui Yang et.al.
2606.12318.authors: Minghui Yang, Ling Guo, Liu Yang
